summaryText: Pastor Mike Kai, a guest speaker at Impact Church, preached from Mark chapter 2 on the story of the paralyzed man lowered through the roof by his friends. He opened by sharing his personal testimony of coming to Christ as a young, struggling single father, brought to church by a persistent friend. The message centered on the 'through the roof' faith needed to overcome obstacles and bring people to Jesus. Pastor Mike emphasized that we often come to God seeking healing for our circumstances, but His first priority is to forgive our sins, which is the greater miracle. He highlighted the importance of being the kind of friend who carries others to Christ, tearing through layers of pain, shame, and fear. The sermon concluded with a call to salvation, during which many in the congregation responded. The core teaching is that authentic faith persists through barriers to bring people to Jesus, where they find forgiveness and a new life that leads to awe and praise.
mainPoints: Through-the-roof faith requires finding a way to win and overcoming every obstacle to get to Jesus.
We often come to God for healing in our circumstances, but His first and greatest work is to forgive our sins.
True faith results in action: we must get off our own mat, walk in the awe of God, and become friends who carry others to Him.

versesMentioned.text: A few days later, when Jesus again entered Capernaum, the people heard that he had come home. They gathered in such large numbers that there was no room left, not even outside the door, and he preached the word to them. Some men came, bringing to him a paralyzed man, carried by four of them. Since they could not get him to Jesus because of the crowd, they made an opening in the roof above Jesus by digging through it and then lowered the mat the man was lying on. When Jesus saw their faith, he said to the paralyzed man, 'Son, your sins are forgiven.' Now some teachers of the law were sitting there, thinking to themselves, 'Why does this fellow talk like that? He’s blaspheming! Who can forgive sins but God alone?' Immediately Jesus knew in his spirit that this was what they were thinking in their hearts, and he said to them, 'Why are you thinking these things? Which is easier: to say to this paralyzed man, ‘Your sins are forgiven,’ or to say, ‘Get up, take your mat and walk’? But I want you to know that the Son of Man has authority on earth to forgive sins.' So he said to the man, 'I tell you, get up, take your mat and go home.' He got up, took his mat and walked out in full view of them all. This amazed everyone and they praised God, saying, 'We have never seen anything like this!'

Monday.scripture: Mark 2:4 - 'Since they could not get him to Jesus because of the crowd, they made an opening in the roof above Jesus by digging through it and then lowered the mat the man was lying on.'
Monday.action: Identify one 'obstacle' (like discouragement or busyness) that is keeping you or someone you know from Jesus. Make one concrete plan to overcome it this week.
Monday.prayer: Lord, give me the persistent, 'through-the-roof' faith of these friends. Help me not to be deterred by the crowd or the difficulty when bringing needs to You.

Tuesday.scripture: Mark 2:5 - 'When Jesus saw their faith, he said to the paralyzed man, ‘Son, your sins are forgiven.’'
Tuesday.action: Spend time in quiet reflection. Thank Jesus that His primary work was to forgive your sins, which is greater than any circumstantial healing.
Tuesday.prayer: Jesus, thank You for forgiving my sins. Help me to value this gift above all else and to find my ultimate peace and identity in Your forgiveness, not just in changed circumstances.

Wednesday.scripture: Mark 2:11-12 - 'He got up, took his mat and walked out in full view of them all. This amazed everyone and they praised God...'
Wednesday.action: Share with one person something God has done in your life that amazes you. It could be forgiveness, provision, or a changed heart.
Wednesday.prayer: God, let my life be a testimony that leaves people amazed and praising You. Fill me with fresh awe for who You are and what You do.

Thursday.scripture: Mark 2:3 - 'Some men came, bringing to him a paralyzed man, carried by four of them.'
Thursday.action: Reach out to one person who might be feeling spiritually 'paralyzed'—lonely, discouraged, or far from God. Send them an encouraging text or invite them to church.
Thursday.prayer: Holy Spirit, show me who needs to be carried to Jesus today. Make me a faithful friend who picks up a corner of the mat and helps bring them to Your presence.

Friday.scripture: Hebrews 10:24-25 - 'And let us consider how we may spur one another on toward love and good deeds, not giving up meeting together...'
Friday.action: If you are part of a small group or church community, actively participate. If not, take a step to connect with one this weekend.
Friday.prayer: Father, thank You for the friends who carried me. Help me to be deeply planted in a faith community where we can carry each other's burdens and spur one another on.

Saturday.scripture: Mark 2:12 - 'This amazed everyone and they praised God, saying, ‘We have never seen anything like this!’'
Saturday.action: Prepare your heart for worship tomorrow. Ask God to do something in your life and in your church service that will amaze you and others.
Saturday.prayer: God, I believe You are still doing amazing things. As I gather with Your people tomorrow, open my eyes to see Your power at work. Let my life and our church be a place where people say, 'We've never seen anything like this before!'
